rag_rules: Use AGREE annex syntax that is valid for contract clauses and keep assumptions/guarantees in component type scope when they refer to component ports.
Generate clauses only from concrete requirement bounds or relations using visible model identifiers.
Only reference symbols that are valid and visible in the current AGREE scope; rely on model-extracted ports and declared names.
generic_rules: Express input envelope constraints as assumptions with explicit lower and upper comparisons when concrete bounds are provided.
Relate outputs only to visible inputs or locally declared helper expressions; avoid implementation-only paths in contract clauses.
Use helper equations only when they simplify a visible boolean relation and their declared type matches the expression type.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Range predicates formed by comparisons are boolean expressions.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed symbols.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths from a component type contract.
Do not create numeric range checks for non-numeric or opaque data without confirmed scalar typing.
Do not use undeclared names, inferred aliases, or non-visible identifiers in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: Implementation-level insertion details for assignments or bindings are not needed for clause generation here.
